claim: MCP standardizes tool/resource/prompt exposure through protocol similar to USB-C analogy, reducing custom integration code
actionable: Replace custom tool integration code with MCP servers for centralized, maintainable capability exposure
source_title: Model Context Protocol Clearly Explained | MCP Beyond the Hype

claim: MCP servers expose three capability types: tools (functions), resources (data/knowledge), prompts (templates)
actionable: Implement list_tools, list_resources, list_prompts handlers when building MCP servers
source_title: Model Context Protocol Clearly Explained | MCP Beyond the Hype

claim: LLMs select tools automatically using schema-based tool descriptions avoiding manual orchestration
actionable: Write detailed MCP tool schemas with descriptions so LLMs select appropriate tools without explicit instruction
source_title: Model Context Protocol Clearly Explained | MCP Beyond the Hype
